content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


October 12, 1993


Charles Kim
Steel Law Building
610 S. Harvard Blvd., Ste. 111
Los Angeles, CA  90005

Re:  Your Request for
     Supplemental Advice
     Our File No. A-93-204a

Dear Mr. Kim:

     You have requested reconsideration of the advice provided to
you on June 17, 1993, concerning the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act.  (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)

     This letter will confirm the telephone advice I provided to
Joseph Ahn on September 2, 1993.  I informed Mr. Ahn that the
advice provided to you on June 17, 1993, was based on the language
of the invitation to Mr. Kwon's retirement party and not on the
activities which occurred at the event.  Therefore, we still
consider the event to be a political fundraiser.

     If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5662.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   [signature]
                                   Carla Wardlow
                                   Division Chief
                                   Technical Assistance Division


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: We still consider the event to be a political fundraiser.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a retirement party event should be classified as a political fundraiser based on the invitation language versus actual activities that occurred at the event.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC maintains its prior advice that the event constitutes a political fundraiser, as the classification is based on the language of the invitation rather than the activities that actually occurred at the event.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. This is a follow-up/supplemental advice letter confirming telephone advice previously provided. Prior advice from June 17, 1993 is referenced but not included in this document. Telephone advice confirmation from September 2, 1993 is summarized.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a retirement party event should be classified as a political fundraiser based on the invitation language versus actual activities that occurred at the event.

CONCLUSION: We still consider the event to be a political fundraiser.

Harvard Blvd., Ste. 111 Los Angeles, CA 90005 Re: Your Request for Supplemental Advice Our File No. A-93-204a Dear Mr. Kim: You have requested reconsideration of the advice provided to you on June 17, 1993, concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.) This letter will confirm the telephone advice I provided to Joseph Ahn on September 2, 1993. I informed Mr. Ahn that the advice provided to you on June 17, 1993, was based on the language of the invitation to Mr. Kwon's retirement party and not on the activities which occurred at the event. Therefore, we still consider the event to be a political fundraiser. If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5662. Sincerely, [signature] Carla Wardlow Division Chief Technical Assistance Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: This is a supplemental advice letter responding to a request for reconsideration of prior advice (dated June 17, 1993) regarding whether a retirement party constitutes a political fundraiser under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC reaffirms its original position that the event is a political fundraiser based on the invitation language.
